{"key_event":"Third Republic institutions and M23 defeat","reasoning":"The 2006 constitutional order, elections, mining revenues, donor/UN backing, and defeat of M23 raised state capacity from the war-transition low, but the FARDC remained fragmented and eastern violence showed only weak territorial control. Civil society gained from churches, NGOs, media, phones, and electoral mobilization, yet repression, disputed elections, activist killings, and armed-group intimidation kept it weak overall.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":3.4,"state_change":1.3,"state_power":3.8}
